name: git-master
tagline: Git workflow skill for atomic commits, interactive rebase, and history archaeology (blame/bisect/pickaxe) — all local git commands.
verdict: No secret access, network exfiltration, or remote code execution found — everything is plain local git commands. Real risks are quality-side: the file is 2x the size budget with duplicated tables, its own commit-count 'hard rules' contradict each other (e.g. '3+ files -> 2+ commits' vs '3 files -> min 1 commit'), it defaults toward history rewrite/force-push

highlights.summary: Force-push and history-rewrite paths ('IF branch already pushed: -> git push --force-with-lease ... ALWAYS use --force-with-lease', lines 816-819; RESET & REBUILD workflow lines 426-440) are gated onl

Heavy fear-based directive language aimed at controlling the consuming agent rather than the user: 'ONE COMMIT = AUTOMATIC FAILURE' (line 33), 'If you're about to make 1 commit ... YOU ARE WRONG' (lin
No negative-trigger guidance anywhere (e.g., not for `git init`/clone/submodules/LFS, not for GitHub PR review) — a user can't tell from the description what git tasks this skill does NOT cover.
